items.name: structuredLogger
items.description: Structured, enterprise-grade request logger. Outputs JSON or pretty-text with consistent fields: `requestId`, `method`, `url`, `status`, `duration`, `ip`, `userAgent`. Correlates with the `requestId` middleware (`req.id`), supports child loggers with bound context, custom transports, and environment-aware log level defaults.

examples.code:
const { structuredLogger } = require('@zero-server/sdk');
  app.use(structuredLogger());

examples.title: JSON Output with Custom Transport
Child Loggers with Bound Context

options.notes: Bound context fields merged into every entry.

methods.description: Create a child logger with additional bound context. Child inherits all parent settings but merges extra fields into every log entry.

methodParams.notes: Extra key-value pairs for the child.

methods.description: Create structured request-logging middleware. Automatically logs every completed request with: `requestId`, `method`, `url`, `status`, `duration`, `ip`, `userAgent`, and `contentLength`. Also attaches `req.log` — a child logger with bound request context so handlers can log with full correlation.

methodOptions.notes: Minimum log level. Default follows NODE_ENV.
Output format. `'json'` for production, `'pretty'` for dev. Default: `'json'` in production, `'pretty'` otherwise.
Custom transport `(entry) => void` for each log entry.
ANSI colors (default: TTY detection). Only applies to pretty format.
`(req, res) => boolean` — skip logging for certain requests.
`(req, res) => object` — extra fields to merge into each request log entry.

methods.example: app.use(structuredLogger());

examples.code: app.use(structuredLogger());
examples.title: Auto-detect Format
JSON to File Transport

examples.code: app.use(structuredLogger({ skip: (req) => req.url === '/healthz' }));
examples.title: Skip Health Checks

items.name: MetricsRegistry
items.description: Zero-dependency metrics registry with Prometheus-compatible text exposition format. Provides Counter, Gauge, and Histogram metric types with label support, automatic HTTP instrumentation middleware, and a handler for `/metrics` endpoints.

examples.title: Quick Setup

examples.code:
scrape_configs:
    - job_name: 'my-app'
      scrape_interval: 5s
      static_configs:
        - targets: ['localhost:3000']
examples.title: prometheus.yml
Custom Metrics

methods.example:
counter.inc();                                    // no labels, +1
  counter.inc({ method: 'GET', status: '200' });   // with labels, +1
  counter.inc({ method: 'POST' }, 5);              // with labels, +5

examples.code:
counter.inc();                                    // no labels, +1
  counter.inc({ method: 'GET', status: '200' });   // with labels, +1
  counter.inc({ method: 'POST' }, 5);              // with labels, +5

methods.description: Set the gauge to a specific value.

methodParams.notes: Label values or value (if no labels).

methods.example:
gauge.set(42);
  gauge.set({ pool: 'primary' }, 10);

examples.code:
gauge.set(42);
  gauge.set({ pool: 'primary' }, 10);

methodParams.notes: Label values or value (if no labels).

methods.example:
histogram.observe(0.235);                         // no labels
  histogram.observe({ method: 'GET' }, 0.042);      // with labels

examples.code:
histogram.observe(0.235);                         // no labels
  histogram.observe({ method: 'GET' }, 0.042);      // with labels

methods.description: Start a timer that, when stopped, observes the elapsed duration in seconds.

methodReturns.description: Stop function — call it to record the duration.

methods.description: Get summary stats for a label combination.

methods.example: const c = registry.counter({ name: 'http_requests_total', help: 'Total HTTP requests', labels: ['method', 'status'] });

examples.code: const c = registry.counter({ name: 'http_requests_total', help: 'Total HTTP requests', labels: ['method', 'status'] });

methods.example:
const g = registry.gauge({ name: 'process_memory_bytes', help: 'Memory usage',
      collect: (gauge) => gauge.set(process.memoryUsage().heapUsed) });

examples.code:
const g = registry.gauge({ name: 'process_memory_bytes', help: 'Memory usage',
      collect: (gauge) => gauge.set(process.memoryUsage().heapUsed) });

methods.example: const h = registry.histogram({ name: 'http_request_duration_seconds', help: 'Request duration', labels: ['method', 'route'] });

examples.code: const h = registry.histogram({ name: 'http_request_duration_seconds', help: 'Request duration', labels: ['method', 'route'] });

methods.description: Get a registered metric by name.
Reset all metric values without removing registrations.
Serialize all metrics to Prometheus text exposition format.
Return all metrics as a plain object (for JSON export or IPC transfer).
Merge a metrics snapshot (from `toJSON()`) into this registry. Used for aggregating worker metrics on the primary process.
Create the standard set of HTTP metrics on a registry.

methodReturns.description: Object with all default metric instances.

methods.description: Create HTTP metrics collection middleware. Automatically tracks `http_requests_total`, `http_request_duration_seconds`, and `http_active_connections`.

methodOptions.notes: Metrics registry. Creates a new one if not provided.
`(req) => string` — extract route label for metrics. Default: `req.route || req.url`.
`(req) => boolean` — skip metrics for certain requests.

methods.description: Create a metrics endpoint handler. Returns Prometheus text exposition format.
methods.example: app.get('/metrics', metricsEndpoint(registry));

examples.code: app.get('/metrics', metricsEndpoint(registry));

items.name: Tracer
items.description: Zero-dependency distributed tracing with W3C Trace Context propagation. Provides span creation, context propagation via `traceparent`/`tracestate` headers, and auto-instrumentation middleware for HTTP, ORM queries, WebSocket, SSE, gRPC, and outbound fetch calls. Compatible with OpenTelemetry: spans export in OTLP-like format and support configurable exporters for Jaeger, Zipkin, or any custom backend.

methods.example:
span.setAttribute('http.method', 'GET');
  span.setAttribute('http.status_code', 200);

examples.code:
span.setAttribute('http.method', 'GET');
  span.setAttribute('http.status_code', 200);

methods.description: Add a timestamped event to the span.
methods.example: span.addEvent('cache.miss', { key: 'user:123' });

examples.code: span.addEvent('cache.miss', { key: 'user:123' });

methods.description: Record an exception as a span event and set error status.
End the span and report to the tracer.
Duration in milliseconds (or null if not ended).
The traceparent header value for this span.

methodOptions.notes: Trace ID (inherits from parent context or generates new).

methods.description: Whether a new trace should be sampled.
Register a listener for completed spans.
Flush buffered spans to the exporter.
Shut down the tracer, flushing remaining spans.
Create HTTP tracing middleware. Automatically creates a span for each request, extracts incoming `traceparent`/`tracestate` headers, and sets outgoing `traceparent`.

methodOptions.notes: Tracer instance. Creates a default if not provided.
`(req) => string` — extract route label for span name.
`(req) => boolean` — skip tracing for certain requests.
Propagate W3C trace context via response headers.

methods.example: app.use(tracingMiddleware({ tracer }));

examples.code: app.use(tracingMiddleware({ tracer }));

methods.description: Instrument outbound fetch calls with tracing. Wraps the zero-server fetch to inject `traceparent` headers and create client spans.

items.name: healthCheck
items.description: Health check middleware with liveness and readiness probes. Kubernetes-compatible `/healthz` and `/readyz` endpoints with composable checks (database ping, memory, event loop lag, disk space) and custom check registration. Returns `200` when healthy, `503` when degraded or during shutdown drain phase, with a JSON body detailing each check.

examples.title: Using the App Integration

methods.description: Create a health check route handler. Returns a JSON response with the status of all registered checks. Returns `200` when all checks pass, `503` when any check fails or when the application is in drain/shutdown state. Response format: ```json { "status": "healthy", "uptime": 12345, "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z", "checks": { "database": { "healthy": true, "duration": 5, "details": {} } } } ```

methodOptions.notes: Named check functions. Each returns `{ healthy, details }` or a boolean/Promise.
Max time to wait for all checks in ms.
`(results) => void` — called when any check fails.

methods.example: app.get('/healthz', healthCheck());

examples.code: app.get('/healthz', healthCheck());

examples.title: Auto-deregister from Load Balancer

methods.description: Create paired liveness and readiness handlers for an app. Liveness includes basic process checks; readiness includes all registered dependency checks.
Check event loop lag against a threshold.
Check available disk space (simple heuristic using os.freemem).
